children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director of the Bureau of Land Management, shall, subject to valid existing rights, manage the conservation area to conserve, protect, and enhance the resources described in , in accordance with this subchapter, the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 [ et seq.], and other applicable laws. The Secretary shall only allow such uses of the conservation area as he finds will further the purposes for which the conservation area is established.section 460ccc–1 of this title43 U.S.C. 1701
Subject to paragraph (2), the Secretary shall permit hunting within the conservation area in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada.

The Secretary, after consultation with the Nevada Department of Wildlife, may issue regulations designating zones where and establishing when hunting shall not be permitted for reasons of public safety, administration, or public use and enjoyment.

children.heading: Preventive measures
children.label: c Preventive measures
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall preclude such measures as the Secretary deems necessary to prevent devastating fire or infestation of insects or disease within the conservation area.

children.heading: Mechanized vehicles
children.label: d Mechanized vehicles
children.text: Except when needed for administrative or emergency purposes, the use of mechanized vehicles in the conservation area shall be allowed only on roads and trails specifically designated for such use as provided in the management plan prepared pursuant to .section 460ccc–3 of this title

children.heading: Limits on visitation and use
children.label: e Limits on visitation and use
children.text: The Secretary may limit visitation and use of the conservation area as the Secretary finds appropriate for the protection of the resources of the conservation area.
